desc: Appletree Cove • Marine and mudflat habitats on quiet bay • American and Eurasian Wigeon, gulls • Public fishing pier, visitors dock, walkway viewing sites
accessibility: Parking: Free lot on site | Wheelchair Access: Paved walkway along waterfront | Restrooms: Yes, year-round | Terrain: Flat paved paths and pier | Dogs Allowed: Yes, on leash

desc: Marine and slough habitats • Opposite side of Appletree Cove with added view into Carpenter Creek slough • Portable toilet, small parking area.
accessibility: Parking: Small gravel lot | Wheelchair Access: Limited — uneven surfaces | Restrooms: Portable toilet on site | Terrain: Gravel path, some uneven ground | Dogs Allowed: Yes, on leash

desc: A designated IBA (Important Bird Area) • Extensive views of Admiralty Inlet • Marine, sandy beach, marsh, and wooded habitats • Best known for alcids, loons and cormorants, as well as large numbers of Bonaparte's and Heermann's gulls in the fall • Viewing platform, trails and beach access • Portable toilet, limited handicap access.
accessibility: Parking: Free lot on site | Wheelchair Access: Limited — viewing platform accessible, beach is not | Restrooms: Portable toilet on site | Terrain: Mixed — paved near lighthouse, sandy beach, wooded trail | Dogs Allowed: Yes, on leash

desc: Aquatic and wooded habitats, open fields • Hooded Merganser and Pied-billed Grebe, warblers in migration • Extensive trail system called the Hansville Greenway
accessibility: Parking: Free lot at trailhead | Wheelchair Access: PLACEHOLDER | Restrooms: PLACEHOLDER | Terrain: Forested trails, some uneven ground | Dogs Allowed: Yes, on leash

desc: A short distance away, next to the Hansville general store, Norwegian County Park is fairly new. Like nearby Point-No-Point, it looks across the Admiralty Straits toward Whidbey Island, and gets much the same marine activity. Rafts of birds wander back and forth, so it is worth checking out both view locations. The Hansville Greenway links this area with trails through Buck Lake County Park, where Ospreys nest in the summer and Eagles reign in winter.
accessibility: Parking: Small roadside lot | Wheelchair Access: PLACEHOLDER | Restrooms: PLACEHOLDER | Terrain: Flat grassy bluff with beach access | Dogs Allowed: Yes, on leash

desc: Marine habitat of the Hood Canal, wooded slopes, open lawns, bluff viewing platform, small park, no facilities
accessibility: Parking: Street parking available | Wheelchair Access: Bluff viewpoint is accessible | Restrooms: None on site | Terrain: Flat lawn and bluff overlook | Dogs Allowed: Yes, on leash
